content: Just as biological and chemical agents from the traditional definition of 'agent' produce bounded, context-specific effects limited by their environmental concentration and reactivity, AI agents' working memory capacity limits are not a design bug but an evolved functional feature that prevents unconstrained, disruptive cascading reasoning processes analogous to uncontrolled biological agent spread. This bounded effect design can be intentionally leveraged to build more controllable, goal-aligned AI agents that avoid harmful off-task speculation.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
